summary: Burglary complaints were roughly flat from FY22 to FY23 before declining through FY25, which NYPD's Mayor's Management Report attributes broadly to citywide deterrence, intelligence-driven policing and swift-apprehension efforts across major felony crime categories. The much lower FY26 figure in this dataset reflects only part of the fiscal year rather than a full 12 months, so it is not directly comparable to earlier full-year totals.

sources.label: NYPD, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report
